MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Continued To Mar-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. GERALD CLAUSEN, TEL. NO. 391-4475 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT WALDSMITH OF ABRAMSON SMITH WALDSMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 421-7995 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRIS GARRETT OF GUISCHARD TENG & PORTELLO APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO MARKET CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Defendants met their burden of production by making a prima facie showing that there are no triable issues of material fact. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) This shifted the burden to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. (Id.) Plaintiff has met this burden by establishing that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the harm of this sort was foreseeable. Although foreseeability is not the only factor in determining duty, it "plays a very significant role" in the analysis. (Lawson v. Safeway (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 400, 409.) While duty is a question of law, the issue of foreseeability is a question of fact. (Bigbee v. Pacific Tel. & Tel. Co. (1983) 34 Cal.3d 49, 56.) It may be decided as a question of law only if, "under the undisputed facts there is no room for a reasonable difference of opinion." (Id., citing Schrimscher v. Bryson (1976) 58 Cal.App.3d 660, 664.) Here, Plaintiff has established there is a reasonable difference of opinion. Plaintiff has produced evidence showing that cars drove over the "island," cars had run off of the road and onto the "island" at least twice previously, and Defendants considered measures to prevent cars from entering the "island." (DMF no. 23; AMF nos. 37 and 45.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/ehg
